data.title: Oshkosh jumps to four-month high after issuing upbeat financial targets
data.description:
Oshkosh (NYSE:OSK) +7.1% in Thursday's trading to its highest since February after forecasting strong growth in revenues and adjusted earnings per share over the next three years, underpinned by a robust backlog and sustained demand across its end markets.

In an Investor Day update, Oshkosh (NYSE:OSK) issued guidance for revenues of $13B-$14B in 2028, up from $10.76B in FY 2024 and well above analyst consensus expectations of $10.3B this year, $10.85B in 2026 and $11.5B in 2027.

The company also targeted adjusted EPS of $18.00-$20.00 in 2028, after posting $11.74 in 2024 and far above analyst consensus for $10.20 this year, $11.95 in 2026 and $13.92 in 2027.

Oshkosh (OSK) said it is growing strong resilient segments to support balanced returns, and expects the Vocational segment's contribution to adjusted operating income will be on par with its Access segment by 2028, while the delivery vehicle business also is growing, and defense margins are expected to improve with new economic price adjustment provisions.

After a period of elevated new product and capital spending, the company said it expects to generate significant free cash flow and attractive free cash flow conversion, and is committed to returning cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

data.title: Toro adjusts 2025 EPS guidance to $4.15–$4.30 amid residential headwinds and tariff mitigation actions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Toro Company (TTC) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Richard M. Olson stated the company “exceeded our adjusted earnings per share expectation for the quarter, took decisive actions to overcome near-term headwinds, executed on our playbook to mitigate tariffs, and we continue to introduce new, innovative products and solutions that enhance customer productivity at a time when it's needed most.”
  • Olson highlighted that adjusted EPS grew to $1.42 in the second quarter, noting continued cash returns to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. He acknowledged “revenue in the quarter declined 2.3% year-over-year to $1.3 billion as weak consumer confidence, coupled with a late spring in many regions, created near-term headwinds for products sold to homeowners.”
  • Product innovation remained a focus, as Olson pointed to successful launches, particularly in the Golf and Grounds business, and highlighted new electric construction equipment and smart connected solutions.
  • Olson discussed operational actions, including “winding down production in one of our plants in Mexico and transitioning that production to existing facilities in the United States” to improve efficiency and cost absorption.
  • CFO Angela C. Drake commented, “We were pleased to deliver adjusted diluted EPS growth in the quarter, highlighted by professional segment growth and profitability improvement.”

Outlook

  • Drake provided updated guidance: “Looking ahead to the third quarter of fiscal 2025, we anticipate total company net sales to be flat to slightly up compared to the prior year. We expect professional segment net sales to be up mid-single digits and residential segment net sales to be down high teens compared to the same period last year.”
  • For the full year, management now expects “total year revenue will be flat to down 3% from fiscal 2024. We expect the professional segment to be up slightly year-over-year while revenue from the residential segment is expected to be down mid-teens.”
  • Guidance for adjusted diluted EPS was revised: “We are slightly reducing our range for adjusted diluted EPS to be $4.15 to $4.30, which at midpoint implies year-over-year growth of 1% even with a likely decline in revenue.”
  • The company continues to expect “total company adjusted gross margins and adjusted operating earnings as a percentage of net sales to improve on a year-over-year basis.”

Financial Results

  • Olson reported that adjusted EPS for the quarter was $1.42. Drake specified “consolidated net sales for the quarter were $1.32 billion, down slightly from Q2 last year.”
  • Professional segment net sales were “just over $1 billion, up about 1% year-over-year,” and earnings for the segment were $202 million, “up 6% year-over-year.”
  • Residential segment net sales were $297 million, “down 11% year-over-year,” with segment earnings of $16 million compared to $36 million last year.
  • Reported and adjusted gross margins were 33.1% and 33.4%, respectively. Adjusted operating earnings margin was 13.7%.
  • Free cash flow through the second quarter was $84.7 million. The company deployed $100 million towards share repurchases during the quarter, totaling $200 million year-to-date.

Q&A

  • Timothy Ronald Wojs, Baird, asked about landscape business inventory levels and performance. Olson replied that field inventory has “largely returned to where we would like to be in that category,” though there was some higher inventory due to the delayed start to spring.
  • Wojs inquired about the residential business as a driver of the guidance revision. Olson confirmed: “It's the residential business, but the reason why we use the word homeowner is because it's the residential business plus that portion of our landscape contractor business that is sold to homeowners.” He cited macroeconomic factors and a slow spring as primary drivers.
  • Samuel John Darkatsh, Raymond James, questioned tariff exposures and mitigation. Olson explained that China remains the largest category, with steel and aluminum also significant, but noted the company plans to “mitigate all of that -- virtually all of that through our actions this year.” Drake confirmed, “Yes, that's going to be for fiscal 2025.”
  • David Sutherland MacGregor, Longbow Research, probed about price/cost dynamics and sell-through trends. Drake stated, “For Q2, our price will be slightly up, really driven kind of by less promotion and incentives in our residential space, and our cost was more.” Olson noted, “Retail is good. To answer your question, in May, much better than it was in April. Now into June continues to be solid.”
  • Edward Randolph Jackson, Northland Capital Markets, asked about working capital and inventory levels. Drake said, “We are a little higher on finished goods, driven by the late spring that we've been talking about. But sequentially, we're actually down in net inventory, about 1%.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was neutral to slightly negative, focusing on concerns regarding guidance reductions, residential demand, inventory, and tariff impacts. Questions were consistently probing on downside risk.
  • Management maintained a confident but cautious tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing resilience and proactive mitigation. During Q&A, responses were factual and at times defensive, with phrases like “To be clear...” and “We believe...” signaling measured optimism and awareness of challenges.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts’ tone was more pressing on macro headwinds and tariff risks, while management shifted from the prior quarter’s more assertive outlook to one of caution and contingency planning.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full-year revenue shifted from growth of zero to 1% in Q1 to a new range of flat to down 3% in Q2. The adjusted diluted EPS guidance was lowered from $4.25–$4.40 to $4.15–$4.30.
  • Management’s strategic focus has moved further toward cost mitigation and operational adjustments, including production shifts and workforce reductions, compared to the previous quarter’s focus on innovation and product launches.
  • Analysts in the current call concentrated more on residential weakness and the magnitude of tariff headwinds, whereas the prior quarter featured more questions on inventory normalization and AMP productivity gains.
  • Management’s confidence remains, but current tone reflects increased caution, particularly regarding homeowner demand and external cost pressures.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management repeatedly cited macroeconomic headwinds, especially high interest rates and weak consumer confidence, affecting residential and homeowner-related sales.
  • Tariff exposure remains a key risk, with management estimating tariffs at 3% of annual cost of goods sold, but stating “we plan to mitigate all of that -- virtually all of that through our actions this year.”
  • Inventory valuation adjustments and lower battery adoption rates were highlighted as additional challenges within the residential segment.

Final Takeaway

The Toro Company delivered adjusted EPS ahead of expectations in the second quarter and remains focused on operational excellence, innovation, and proactive cost management. However, management revised full-year guidance downward to reflect continued macro headwinds in the residential segment and the impact of tariffs. Strategic actions, including manufacturing shifts and productivity initiatives, are expected to mitigate most cost pressures, while the company’s professional segment continues to show resilience and drive profitability. Management remains confident in its ability to navigate challenges and create long-term value, supported by robust product innovation and strong cash generation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Medicare cited as a potential target in Trump tax bill
data.description:
Senate Republicans are said to be eyeing potential funding cuts to Medicare, a government-backed health insurance program mainly for seniors, as they race to pass President Donald Trump’s signature tax and spending bill, multiple media outlets reported Thursday.

As the lower house of the U.S. Congress narrowly cleared the legislative package dubbed “The One, Big, Beautiful Bill” last month, House Republicans largely avoided touching the program to pay for massive tax cuts proposed in the bill.

Instead, Medicaid, a healthcare program jointly funded by the government and states, was their target as they passed their version of the bill, which, according to the Congressional Budget Office, could leave nearly 11M Americans without insurance by 2034.

“I think the focus, as you know, has been on addressing waste, fraud, and abuse in Medicaid,” Politico reported, quoting Senate Majority Leader John Thune as saying on Thursday.

“But right now we’re open to suggestions if people have them about other areas where there is clearly waste, fraud, and abuse that can be rooted out in any government program,” Thune added.

However, citing people familiar with the debate, Bloomberg News reported that Senate Republicans are not yet fully onboard with plans to bring changes to privately run Medicare Advantage plans, which could potentially lead to funding cuts for companies operating in that market.

Health insurers: UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Humana (NYSE:HUM), CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), Cigna (CI), Clover Health (CLOV), Alignment Healthcare (NASDAQ:ALHC), Centene (NYSE:CNC), Molina Healthcare (NYSE:MOH), Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV),

data.title: Chinese services firms optimistic for trade deal, but IBKR is 'increasingly pessimistic'
data.description:
Latest data from Caixin and S&P Global showed China's services sector expanding at a faster pace in May from April, with surveyed companies expressing hope for positive trade developments between the world's two largest economies. However, Interactive Brokers is "increasingly pessimistic" for a trade deal. 

Early in the morning, Caixin and S&P Global's headline measure of services business activity came in at 51.1 for May, a rise from 50.7 in April. Incoming new business expanded at the second-fastest pace in YTD.

The M/M improvement comes at a time of elevated trade tensions between China and the U.S.

President Donald Trump on Thursday said he had held a "very good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping which "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the two nations. Trump last week had accused China of violating a temporary truce reached in May. 

“Business optimism improved. In May, although the indicator for future activity expectations remained below its historical average, the reading ticked up by just over 1 point. Surveyed companies were hopeful that the negative impact of the U.S. tariffs may ease in the future,” Wang Zhe, senior economist at Caixin, said. 

"An increase in new orders supported business momentum and hiring, but cost pressures did climb significantly. The inflationary impulse was seen as temporary, however, and didn’t weigh on optimism since firms in aggregate reported confidence of an incoming trade deal," José Torres, senior economist at Interactive Brokers, said on Thursday. 

"Quite honestly, though, I’m increasingly pessimistic about a cross-border commerce agreement between Washington and Beijing and believe that elevated tensions will remain the norm for years to come," the economist said.

"Trump’s onshoring goals essentially require a reduction in China’s share of international manufacturing production. That’s too much of a sacrifice for the Chinese Communist Party, considering that it’s been their growth engine for decades," Torres added.

Wall Street on Thursday seesawed as traders digested Trump and Xi's phone call and a public spat between the U.S. President and Elon Musk. 

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

And here are some China-linked ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:FLCH), (BATS:CNYA), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), and (NYSEARCA:YINN).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Rocket Companies prices private offering of notes due 2030, 2033
data.description:
Fintech platform Rocket Companies (NYSE:RKT) announced on Thursday the pricing of a private offering of $2.0B 6.125% senior notes due 2030 and $2.0B 6.375% senior notes due 2033.

The notes will initially be fully and unconditionally guaranteed on a senior unsecured basis by the unit Rocket Mortgage.

Upon the consummation of the Redfin (RDFN) acquisition, the notes will also be guaranteed, on a senior unsecured basis, by Redfin. Additionally, the notes will also be guaranteed on a senior unsecured basis by Mr. Cooper (COOP) upon the consummation of the Mr. Cooper acquisition.

The offering is expected to close on June 20.

The proceeds are intended to be used to redeem certain notes issued by the Mr. Cooper unit Nationstar Mortgage Holdings, pay fees and expenses related to the offering and redemption, and repay secured debt.

Shares were +3.85% during afternoon trading to $12.95 as Redfin stockholders approved the company's proposed sale to Rocket.

data.title: Excelerate Energy started at Buy at Jefferies on LNG exposure with minimal commodity risk
data.description:
Excelerate Energy (NYSE:EE) +4.4% in Thursday's trading as Jefferies initiated coverage of the liquefied natural gas storage and transportation firm with a Buy rating and $39 price target.

Excelerate (NYSE:EE) offers a unique opportunity for exposure to long-term liquefied natural gas demand tailwinds through its FSRU and regas infrastructure that has stable contracted cash flows and minimal commodity risk, and the company is capitalizing by growing its asset base and enhancing integration with a FSRU newbuild, LNG SPA contracts, and acquiring New Fortress Energy's Jamaican LNG-to-power assets, Jefferies analyst Emma Schwartz wrote, adding that as growth capex rolls off, free cash flow inflects higher and should offer upside to valuation and return of capital.

Schwartz and her team forecast Excelerate (EE) to generate ~$428M in EBITDA in 2025, which should continue to grow from Jamaica, LNG SPAs, and start-up of Hull 3407; free cash flow is seen declining in 2026 due to the higher capex from Hull 3407, but once this capex rolls off, FCF inflects to ~$236M in 2027 and grows on FSRU recontracting, which will result in ample
headroom to increase its modest dividend, which Jefferies believes could be increased by $90M in 2027.

data.title: Nintendo Switch 2, featuring custom Nvidia chip, launches today with fever-pitch demand
data.description:
Nintendo's (OTCPK:NTDOY)(OTCPK:NTDOF) highly anticipated Switch 2 launched today, with major retailers quickly selling out of the gaming console, which is powered by a custom chip from Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA).

"If the Switch 2 matches or exceeds the success of the original—which sold over 150 million units—suppliers like NVIDIA, which provides the custom Tegra 239 chip, could see a boost in their embedded systems business, even though it remains a much smaller revenue segment compared to data centers or gaming GPUs," said Seeking Alpha analyst Bernard Zambonin.

The Switch 2 also features UFS storage provided by SK hynix (OTCPK:HXSCF) and a Wi-fi/Bluetooth receiver from MediaTek (OTCPK:MDTK.F). 

Nvidia said the new console enables up to 4K gaming in TV mode and up to 120 FPS at 1080p in handheld mode. The device also supports high dynamic range, or HDR, and AI upscaling to sharpen visuals and smooth gameplay. It features 10 times the graphics performance of the first Switch.

"Nvidia content appears to be most abundant in the Nintendo Switch 2," said Seeking Alpha analyst Michael Del Monte. "Comparing the specs between the Nintendo Switch OLED and Switch 2, I have reason to believe the console may not have as big of a splash in the market as one may think, particularly when comparing display quality. Though Nvidia may be providing more content in the new device, the Switch 2 release shouldn't be expected to be a needle-mover for Nvidia given the muted impact when compared to its Data Center market."

Nintendo said it expects to sell 15M units during the fiscal year that ends in March 2026, but analysts believe that's a conservative estimate. 

"Despite tariff challenges, Nintendo's strategic pricing and accessory adjustments should mitigate impacts, and I expect 17M units to be sold by March 2026," writes Seeking Alpha analyst Compounding Chef.

The original Switch ranks as the third best-selling gaming console of all time, ranking behind Sony's (SONY) PlayStation 2 and the Nintendo DS.  

CNET's Switch 2 Stock Tracker shows the console is already sold out at Walmart (WMT) and GameStop (GME). It is available in-store or in select stores only at Best Buy (BBY), Target (TGT) and Staples.

data.title: Newegg skyrockets on high volume and is now up for the year
data.description:
Newegg Commerce (NASDAQ:NEGG) soared 59% in Thursday trading and crossed over the $10 level for the first time since early January.

The retailer did not post any press releases or SEC filings that would account for the buying surge. Some traders are pointing to the name as having attracted momentum speculators. 

Volume on Newegg (NASDAQ:NEGG) was more than 10X normal activity, with more than two hours of trading still left in the session. Short interest on Newegg (NEGG) stands at 5.7% of the total float.

Newegg (NEGG) regained compliance with the Nasdaq in April after a reverse stock split boosted its share price.

data.title: PVH adjusts full-year EPS target to $10.75-$11 and projects 8.5% operating margin amid tariff and promotional pressure
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: PVH Corp. (PVH) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Stefan Larsson began by highlighting that PVH "grew revenue 2% above our guidance, and we delivered stronger-than-expected non-GAAP EPS, also above our guidance" for the quarter. He credited the PVH+ Plan, emphasizing disciplined execution in both Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger brands and noted, "we remain on track to drive revenue growth for the full year."
  • Larsson detailed iconic product launches and marketing: Calvin Klein's new icon cotton stretch underwear for men, featuring Bad Bunny, delivered a "25% increase in combined sales of Icon Cotton Stretch and Cotton Stretch styles globally." Calvin Klein's fashion denim grew "14% in the quarter" due to expanded offerings. Tommy Hilfiger drove performance through new summer campaigns and partnerships, including a fresh Formula 1 alignment with Cadillac.
  • Larsson discussed regional dynamics, stating Europe saw "overall revenue increasing mid-single digits," and in the Americas, "overall revenue increased high single digits, above our plan," while Asia Pacific revenues "declined low teens on a reported basis."
  • Larsson announced the appointment of David Savman as Global Brand President for Calvin Klein, highlighting his role in addressing operational challenges.
  • CFO Zachary James Coughlin reported, "revenue for the first quarter was up 2% on both a reported and constant currency basis." He cited regional growth in EMEA (up 4% in constant currency), Americas (up 7%), and a decline in Asia Pacific (down 11% in constant currency). Coughlin stated, "EPS came in slightly ahead of guidance driven by lower tax and interest expense."

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed "full year revenue outlook, but we are updating our earnings outlook to reflect our revised expectations for the remainder of the year with 3 main changes versus our prior guidance."
  • Coughlin projected, "our full year operating margin is now projected to be approximately 8.5%, and EPS is projected to be in the range of $10.75 to $11."
  • The company expects a "gross margin ... decrease approximately 250 basis points versus last year. Our previous guidance was a decrease of approximately 100 basis points."
  • Tariffs are expected to create a "headwind of approximately $65 million to our full year EBIT weighted dominantly in the second half of the year."
  • For Q2, Coughlin guided, "second quarter operating margin to be approximately 6.5% to 7%" and "earnings per share is expected to be in a range of $1.85 to $2."

Financial Results

  • PVH reported "operating margin of 8.1% within our guidance range as we navigated an increasingly promotional environment."
  • "EBIT for the quarter was $160 million, and operating margin was 8.1%. Earnings per share was $2.30. Interest expense was $17 million, and our tax rate for the quarter was approximately 17%."
  • The company took a "noncash goodwill and other intangible asset impairment charge of $480 million, which was primarily due to an increase in discount rates."
  • Inventory at quarter end was "up 19% compared to Q1 last year," primarily due to investment in core product categories and earlier receipts. SG&A as a percent of revenue was "50.5%, a 90 basis point improvement versus last year."
  • PVH returned "over $550 million to shareholders during the quarter with the repurchase of 5.4 million shares."

Q&A

  • Jay Daniel Sole, UBS: Questioned confidence behind brand momentum amid lower traffic and increased promotions. Larsson responded that product innovation and focused marketing—such as the 25% growth in Calvin Klein underwear—demonstrate "100% correlated to where we lean in and execute."
  • Michael Charles Binetti, Evercore: Asked about cost-out efforts and operational volatility. Larsson explained that the Calvin Klein operational challenges are being resolved and improvements are expected in fall '25 and spring '26, supported by new leadership. Coughlin affirmed, "we're still on track to deliver between 200 and 300 basis points of SG&A leverage," with 200 basis points delivered by Q4.
  • Dana Lauren Telsey, Telsey Group: Inquired about tariff impact and mitigation. Larsson and Coughlin cited a $65 million headwind and outlined supply chain optimization, targeted pricing, and discount reductions as mitigation strategies.
  • Brooke Siler Roach, Goldman Sachs: Probed about pricing power versus promotional activity. Larsson emphasized scaling PVH+ execution in product innovation and marketing, especially in key categories, and increasing marketing spend to drive traffic.
  • Matthew Robert Boss, JPMorgan: Asked about top-line and margin trajectory. Larsson attributed margin pressure to macro headwinds, especially in North America and China, but expects improvement in the back half due to cost savings and operational resolution. Coughlin detailed that incremental gross margin decline is "50 basis points ... due to the mitigated impact of tariffs" and "100 basis points ... an increase in the promotionality."
  • John David Kernan, TD Cowen: Queried about promotional impact in H2. Coughlin projected "around 100 basis points tied to the increased promotional environment," with no expectation of sequential improvement in promotional activity through year-end.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts conveyed a slightly negative tone, especially around traffic trends, promotional intensity, margin headwinds, and confidence in cost mitigation and brand momentum.
  • Management maintained a confident stance in prepared remarks, but adopted a more defensive and pragmatic tone in Q&A, particularly when addressing macro headwinds and promotional pressures. Larsson used phrases like "not yet in a position to fully mitigate the macro headwinds."
  • Compared to the prior quarter, both management and analysts displayed increased caution, with more focus on risk mitigation and operational challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from "flat to up slightly" on EBIT margin and record EPS to lowered targets: "operating margin ... approximately 8.5%, and EPS ... $10.75 to $11."
  • Management highlighted new macro challenges, including tariffs and intensified promotions, as the primary drivers behind margin and EPS reductions.
  • Analysts' questions moved from optimism about order book growth and cost initiatives to concerns over sustainability of momentum and effectiveness of mitigation strategies.
  • The tone has become more cautious and defensive, with operational issues and external risks taking center stage.
  • Strategic focus moved from growth and product launches to margin protection and cost control.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited "highly dynamic and uncertain macroenomic environment globally," significant "uncertainty around global trade policies," and weakening consumer sentiment, particularly in the U.S. and China.
  • Tariffs projected to have a $65 million unmitigated EBIT impact, mostly in the second half.
  • Operational challenges in Calvin Klein's global product creation remain a headwind, with improvement expected into 2026.
  • Promotional environment is expected to persist, pressuring gross margin by approximately 100 basis points for the year.
  • Analysts remain concerned about the effectiveness and timing of mitigation actions and brand resilience in the current environment.

Final Takeaway

PVH delivered Q1 results above guidance for revenue and EPS, but management revised its full-year outlook downward for both operating margin and EPS due to ongoing macro headwinds, persistent promotional pressure, and tariff impacts. The company is intensifying its focus on product innovation, marketing investment, and operational improvements, particularly in Calvin Klein, while targeting mitigation of external risks. Management remains committed to sequential improvement and expects to exit 2025 with double-digit operating margins, setting the foundation for stronger profit growth in 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Amazon Web Services slashes prices for Amazon EC2 NVIDIA GPU-accelerated instances
data.description:
• Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) Web Services said on Thursday that it is slashing prices for its Amazon EC2 NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA) GPU-accelerated instances by up to 45%.
  • “Today, we’re announcing up to 45 percent price reduction for Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) NVIDIA GPU-accelerated instances: P4 (P4d and P4de) and P5 (P5 and P5en) instance types,” AWS said on a blog post. “This price reduction to On-Demand and Savings Plan pricing applies to all Regions where these instances are available. The pricing reduction applies to On-Demand purchases beginning June 1 and to Savings Plan purchases effective after June 4.”
  • The cloud computing giant cut prices for instance types that incorporate Nvidia's A100, H100 and H200 GPUs, with discounts depending upon the length of EC2 and compute usage plans.
